返回本批次

新购买的 Vizio 电视现在需要沃尔玛帐户才能使用智能功能

Newly purchased Vizio TVs now require Walmart accounts to use smart features

arstechnica.com 作者 vidyesh 分数 176 评论 169 2026/03/26 22:34
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 沃尔玛广告工具 本周的公告显示，沃尔玛进一步整合 Vizio，并希望更深入地利用 Vizio OS 的广告和跟踪功能来推动其 64 亿美元的广告业务。 Vizio 作为自己公司的最后一个季度，其广告业务毛利润为 1.158 亿美元，硬件业务亏损 670 万美元。沃尔玛没有透露 Vizio 的财务数据，但在 2 月份与投资者的电话会议中，沃尔玛首席财务官兼执行副总裁约翰·大卫·雷尼 (John David Rainey) 表示，在沃尔玛 2026 财年第四季度，Vizio“广告业务实现了三位数增长”。虽然自 2024 年中期（沃尔玛收购之前）以来，Vizio 操作系统就要求使用 Vizio 帐户，但与零售集团相关的帐户对于用于说服消费者的电视具有额外且更明显的影响。用户购买东西——尤其是从沃尔玛购买东西。那些可能容忍在以广告为中心的电视品牌上开设账户的人可能会拒绝在沃尔玛上开设账户。沃尔玛的公告称：“新的简化登录简化了设置，同时建立了跨设备的安全身份框架，将流媒体互动与零售互动直接连接起来。”为了进一步将 Vizio TV 转变为沃尔玛广告工具，沃尔玛本周宣布，Vizio OS 将展示美容品牌欧莱雅的广告，并连接到沃尔玛和其他公司的产品页面。多年来，Vizio 一直在从一个价格稳定的电视品牌转型为一家广告公司。在收购 Vizio 一年多后，沃尔玛准备将 Vizio 彻底推向这个门槛（如果还没有的话）。广告和跟踪在低价电视中几乎无法避免，并且在高端电视中变得越来越普遍，这使得难以捉摸的哑电视和其他智能电视替代品越来越有吸引力。

Original Summary

  1. A Walmart ads vehicle This week’s announcement shows Walmart further integrating Vizio and looking to more deeply leverage Vizio OS’s ads and tracking capabilities to fuel its $6.4 billion ads business. In Vizio’s final quarter as its own company, its ad business made a gross profit of $115.8 million, and its hardware business lost $6.7 million. Walmart doesn’t break out Vizio’s financials, but in a February call with investors, John David Rainey, CFO and EVP of Walmart, said Vizio “saw triple-digit growth in advertising” during Walmart’s fiscal Q4 2026. While Vizio OS has required a Vizio account since mid-2024 (before Walmart’s acquisition), an account connected to a retail conglomerate has additional and more obvious implications for TVs being used to convince users to buy stuff—particularly from Walmart. Those who may have tolerated making an account with an ad-centric TV brand might resist making one with Walmart. “The new, streamlined login simplifies setup while establishing a secure identity framework across devices, connecting streaming engagement directly with retail interaction,” Walmart’s announcement said. Furthering Vizio TVs’ transformation into a Walmart ad vehicle, Walmart announced this week that Vizio OS will show ads for beauty brand L’Oréal that connect to Walmart’s and other companies’ product pages. For years, Vizio has been transforming from a solidly priced TV brand into an ad business. Over a year after buying Vizio, Walmart is poised to completely push Vizio over that threshold, if it isn’t there already. Ads and tracking have become nearly impossible to avoid among lower-priced TVs and are becoming more common in premium options, making the elusive dumb TV and other smart TV alternatives increasingly attractive.

giancarlostoro 2026/03/26 23:32
我从不将智能电视连接到网络，我只是插入 Apple TV 即可继续。这是令人沮丧的，因为打开这些设备所需的时间比应有的时间要长，因为所有额外的开销都是我不想要或不需要的。我希望有人能制作一个轻松的小工具，用可以立即加载的愚蠢软件来刷新软件。
I never connect my smart TVs to the network, I just plug in my Apple TV and move on. It's frustrating because it takes longer to turn these devices on than it should because of all the additional overhead I don't want or need. I wish someone would make a painless gadget to flash the software with dumber software that loads instantly.

bko 2026/03/27 04:28
最疯狂的事情是，如果我没有亲自经历过，我不会相信，但三星会定期在您的手机上安装随机应用程序和游戏。你删除它们，然后一个月后，新的应用程序就会出现。这是我见过的最激进的反消费者行为之一，而且它来自如此大的玩家。
The most insane thing which I wouldn't believe if I hadn't experienced it myself, but Samsung will periodically just install random apps and games on your phone. Loughla 2026/03/27 01:12
我们刚刚买了一辆 vizio 来替换坏掉的那辆。一旦您跳过设置过程，您所需要做的就是从遥控器或其他任何东西上的“主页”按钮上除去焊料，您将永远不会再看到他们弹出的垃圾。

不过，我明白为什么人们喜欢这样。它将您的所有帐户放在一处。老实说，如果我可以通过它运行 Plex 和我自己的媒体，那么我很容易阻止它拨出并摆脱我的 Nvidia 防护罩。

We just bought a vizio to replace one that broke. Once you skip the setup process, all you need to do is remove the solder from the 'home' button on the remote or whatever it is and you'll never see their pop up crap again.

I get why people like this, though. It takes all of your accounts and puts them in one place. Honestly, if I could run Plex and my own media through it, it would be tempting to just block it from dialing out and get rid of my Nvidia shield.

在 HN 中查看
beAbU 2026/03/27 02:05
我有一个 chromecast，其中插入了 google 电视。这些天不知道它叫什么。 Chromecast 主页？

它配备了一个超级简约的遥控器，可以调节音量、方向键、开/关以及可重新映射的 netflix 按钮。我电视原来的遥控器在...某处。我已经一年多没有需要它了。

chromecast 和我的 xbox 都使用 hdmi-cec 来指示电视“将您的输入切换给我”。 chromecast 遥控器直接控制电视音量。

我在网络上有一个 Emby 媒体服务器，我使用 chromecast 上的 emby 应用程序来播放内容。我也是一个销售者，所以我们也有标准的流媒体应用程序。

这是一个真正的神级设置，而且效果非常好。

I have a chromecast with google tv plugged into mine. Not sure what it's called these days. Chromecast Home?

It comes with a super minimalist remote that does volume and a d-pad and on/off and a remappable netflix button. My tv's original remote is... Somewhere. It's been more than a year since I've needed it.

Both the chromecast and my xbox uses hdmi-cec to instruct the tv to "switch your input to me". The chromecast remote controls the tv volume directly.

I have an Emby media server on the network, and I use the emby app on the chromecast to play content. I'm also a sellout so we have the standard streaming apps on there as well.

It's a truly god-tier setup and it works so well.

tombert 2026/03/26 23:47
是的，我的电视根本不连接 WiFi；只要电视有 HDMI 端口，我就会使用 Nvidia Shield TV（或类似的东西）。 如果我这样做了，我就可以访问更多的应用程序、更快捷的界面，并且如果以后需要的话，升级也更容易。

我已经考虑过刷新它以使用更愚蠢的固件，但它进入了我并不能很快理解的技术文档。 自从我成为 Claude Code 会员以来，我就没有研究过它，所以在人工智能的帮助下可能值得重新审视它。

Yeah, I don't connect my TV to WiFi at all; as long as TVs have HDMI ports I'll just use an Nvidia Shield TV (or something similar). If I do that I have access to more apps, a snappier interface, and it's easier to upgrade if I need to later.

I've looked into flashing it to use a dumber firmware, but it got into technical documentation that I don't really understand really quickly. I haven't looked into it since I got a Claude Code membership though, so it might be worth revisiting with AI assistance.

Marsymars 2026/03/27 00:21
我有一台带有 Google TV（未连接到互联网）的索尼电视，开机速度很快。

如果它来自冷启动，例如如果它被拔掉，或者如果它正在执行完全重新启动，则需要更长的时间并显示启动屏幕，但如果它从常规“关闭”状态打开，则它花费的时间与我的其他哑屏幕大致相同，并直接进入上次使用的输入。

I've got a Sony TV with Google TV (not connected to internet) that turns on pretty quickly.

If it's going from a cold boot e.g. where it was unplugged or if it's doing a full reboot it takes a bit longer and shows a splash screen, but if it's turning on from a regular "off" state it takes about the same amount of time as the rest of my dumb screens and goes directly to the last used input.

sjsdaiuasgdia 2026/03/26 22:59
这是假设，现在或以后，屏幕上不会出现一条无法忽略的消息：“登录沃尔玛帐户以启用所有电视功能”。

他们可以通过多种方式干扰以“哑巴”模式使用电视的尝试。哎呀，在您登录之前，它可能会拒绝显示任何视频。

This presumes that, now or later, there won't be an on-screen message that can't be dismissed saying "Sign in to a Walmart account to enable all TV features."

There's plenty of ways they can interfere with attempts to use the TV in "dumb" mode. Heck it could refuse to show any video at all til you've signed in.

jvolkman 2026/03/26 23:11
什么是半屏显示？在我的三星 S90D（去年左右的新产品）上，我可以将其设置为跳过“主”屏幕并直接进入开机时的最后一个输入。效果很好。
What is the half-screen display? On my Samsung S90D (new within the last year or so), I can set it to skip the "home" screen and go directly to the last input on power on. It works well.
在 HN 中查看
sjsdaiuasgdia 2026/03/26 23:34
一个制造商可以针对多个市场，针对不同的市场做出不同的选择。

您购买的三星 S90D（65 英寸 4K 型号）似乎售价约为 1000 美元。查看百思买的网站，您还可以以低至 180 美元的价格购买三星 65 英寸 4K 电视（型号 DU6900）。

是的，还有其他差异。 LED 与 OLED 等。但乍一看，它们似乎等同于消费者……而且成本高出 5 倍。这款售价 1000 美元的电视瞄准的市场对购买的产品期望更高，并且可能会对持续的登录通知感到不满。这款低于 200 美元的电视瞄准的是需要大电视但没有考虑太多的市场。

在某些型号上，三星肯定会更积极地启用智能功能，因为这些型号预计将获得广告补贴。

A manufacturer can target multiple markets and make different choices for different markets.

The Samsung S90D (a 65" 4K model) you bought appears to sell for about $1000. Looking at Best Buy's site, you can also buy a Samsung 65" 4K TV for as little as $180 (model DU6900).

Yes, there's other differences. LED vs OLED etc. But at a glance they seem equivalent to a consumer...and one costs 5x more. The $1000 TV is targeting a market that expects more from their purchase and would potentially grate at a persistent sign-in notification. The <$200 TV is targeting a market that wants a big TV and hasn't thought much past that.

There's definitely a chance that on some models Samsung would be more aggressive about enabling smart features, because those models are expected to be subsidized by ads.

jvolkman 2026/03/26 23:57
虽然所有这些都是正确的，但即使是 DU6900 也有“从 Smart Hub Home 开始”选项，该选项默认启用，但可以根据其手册禁用。我想这就是OP所看到的；在三星电视上禁用它是很常见的事情。
While all of that is certainly true, even the DU6900 has the "Start with Smart Hub Home" option that defaults to enabled but can be disabled according to its manual. I assume that's what OP is seeing; it's a common thing to want to disable on Samsung TVs.

nunez 2026/03/27 01:17
去年我终于亲眼目睹了其中之一！

它既令人着迷又令人恐惧。

它有一系列引人注目的传感器和（我认为？）麦克风，以及中间的一个摄像头。上面是永久的广告展示。电视还可以访问自己的门户，这就是我看到它时正在运行的门户。一位人工智能生成的记者正在谈论娱乐新闻。

就连奥威尔也无法想象比这更好的电幕。你知道它充满了防失败保护措施，以防止好奇的人获得免费电视。

I finally saw one of these in person last year!

It was as fascinating as it was terrifying.

It had a noticable array of sensors and (I think?) microphones along with a camera smack dab in the middle. Above that was its permanent ad display. The TV also has access to its own portal, which was what was on when I saw it. An AI-generated reporter was talking about entertainment news.

Not even Orwell could've imagined a better telescreen than this. You know it's chock full of anti-defeat protections to prevent curious minds from scoring a free TV.

我想知道 SmartTV 黑名单的更改历史和提交规模是否可以讲述事物如何演变的历史故事？ 我知道 DoH 使得这种方法越来越不可行……但对于我现有的电视来说，只要我小心不要盲目更新，它似乎仍然可以正常工作。

Pi-Hole sped up my Samsung home and menu screens tremendously. I use something like this list [0]. The growing size of the list itself is a testament to the enshittification of Smart TVs.

I wonder if the SmartTV blocklist change history and size of commits could tell a historical story of how things have evolved? I'm aware that DoH makes this approach less and less viable ... but for my existing TVs it still seems to work fine as long as I'm careful not to blindly update.

在 HN 中查看
jonpurdy 2026/03/26 23:32
每次我在 HN 上看到与智能电视相关的帖子时，我都会想知道这个问题。我最近购买了一台 LG OLED (C5 48")，因为我的旧电视坏了，所以我最后发表评论。正如其他人所说，只是不要将其连接到互联网。但你已经知道这一点，所以我将提供我的经验轶事，因为我在购买这台电视之前多年来一直想知道同样的事情。

当电视从未连接到互联网，并且您像我一样使用单个 HDMI 源时，电视的行为完全就像一台哑电视。它通过我的 AppleTV 遥控器打开，并在 1-2 秒后显示图片。没有 LG 徽标（我禁用了此功能），也没有显示任何智能界面。

如果你想改变设置，你可以通过LG遥控器显示设置界面，它通常就像一个哑电视（不会挡住整个屏幕，所以你可以调整图像质量并看到预期的结果）。

我的电视已经使用了大约两个月，从未被要求更新或显示任何广告。我唯一一次看到智能全屏界面是当您拔掉实时 HDMI 源并且电视检测到没有任何内容时。 （如果您关闭信号源，它也会告诉电视自行关闭。）

希望这会有所帮助，因为购买一台漂亮的智能电视并这样做比找到一个真正愚蠢的商业面板要容易得多。

I wonder about this every time I see a smart TV-related thread on HN. I recently purchased an LG OLED (C5 48") because my old TV died so I'll finally comment. As others have said, just don't connect it to the internet. But you knew this already, so I'll provide my anecdote on the experience of this since I wondered the same thing for years before getting this TV.

When the TV is never connected to internet, and you use a single HDMI source like me, the TV acts completely like a dumb TV. It gets turned on via my AppleTV remote and displays the picture 1-2 seconds later. No LG logo (I disabled this), and no smart interface shown whatsoever.

If you want to change settings, you can display the settings interface via LG remote control and it generally acts like a dumb TV (not blocking the entire screen, so you can adjust picture quality and see the result as expected).

I've had the TV for about two months and never been asked to update it or shown any ad. The only time I've ever seen the smart fullscreen interface is when you unplug a live HDMI source and the TV detects that nothing is there. (If you turn the source off, it tells the TV to turn itself off as well.)

Hope this helps since it's a lot easier to buy a nice smart TV and do it this way than find a truly dumb commercial panel.

在 HN 中查看
____tom____ 2026/03/27 03:18
不要认为因为您没有将某些东西连接到互联网，所以就没有连接到互联网。

事物可以使用蜂窝调制解调器给家里打电话。这已经完成了。

沃尔玛还可以轻松地与有线电视提供商达成协议，通过 WiFi 进行出站访问，并覆盖全国大部分地区。

Don't assume that because you don't connect something to the internet that is doesn't connect to the internet.

Things can use cellular modems to phone home. This is already done.

Walmart could also easily cut deals with cable providers for outbound access via WiFi and cover most of the country.

在 HN 中查看
0manrho 2026/03/27 04:55
他们还可以与 ISP 达成协议，将他们的电视列入白名单，以访问公共或可能未列出的 WiFi，从而使他们能够以这种方式进行连接，而绝大多数客户都不会意识到。

同样，这些电视可以连接到它可以找到的任何开放的 WiFi 热点，并以这种方式打电话回家/下载更新。例如，Cox 自豪地宣称，任何人（不仅仅是客户/居民）都可以将超过 4M 的住宅客户调制解调器+路由器+AP 用于“WiFi 热点”，只要他们拥有 Cox 帐户。我不明白为什么三星或任何其他制造商可能会以某种“方便”或“无缝更新”为幌子，与上述 ISP 使用该网络来更新设备，表面上是为了那些不太懂技术的用户。

我不知道这些商业交易是否存在，但“智能设备”通常会尝试打电话回家/更新，即使您没有在专用网络上手动配置它。

编辑：忘记了考克斯热点声明的来源： https://www.cox.com/residential/internet/learn/cox-hotspots....

They could also make agreements with ISP's where their TV's can be whitelisted for access to a public or potentially unlisted WiFi, enabling them to connect that way, without the vast majority of customers ever being aware.

Similarly, these TV's could connect to any open wifi hotspot it can find and phone home/download updates that way. Cox for example proudly boasts how more than 4M of it's residential customers modem+router+ap's can be used for "WiFi Hotspots" by anyone - not just the customer/resident - if they have a cox account. I don't see why Samsung or any other manufacturer may approach said ISP's to use this network to update devices under some guise of "convenience" or "seamless updates" ostensibly for their less tech savvy users.

I don't know if these business deals exists, but "smart devices" will often try to phone home/update anyway they can, even if you don't manually configure it on a private network.

EDIT: Forgot the source on the cox hotspots claim: https://www.cox.com/residential/internet/learn/cox-hotspots....

philistine 2026/03/27 01:35

Yeah, if you want a TV that looks terrible. They usually have terrible response times and focus on nits at all costs. Try watching anything HDR on a display panel.
在 HN 中查看
close04 2026/03/26 23:10
大多数也更大、更重、功耗更高，有时最低亮度也很高，令人不舒服。他们很少使用与零售型号相同的面板，因为它们必须支持不同的操作条件，例如极端温度和 24/7 操作。
Most are also larger, heavier, with higher power consumption, and sometimes uncomfortably high minimum brightness. They rarely use the same panels as retail models because they have to support different operating conditions like extreme temperatures and 24/7 operation.
在 HN 中查看
embedding-shape 2026/03/26 23:11
最糟糕的是，通常也更贵，一些面板的成本翻倍，因为目标市场是“广告代理商”或其他什么。
And the worst part, usually way more expensive too, seen some panels where the cost was double because the target market is "ad agencies" or whatever.
在 HN 中查看
close04 2026/03/26 23:23
> 成本翻倍，因为目标市场是“广告代理商”或其他什么。

能够在这些条件下运行的电视必须更昂贵，否则需要两倍的更换频率，并且长期成本会更高。还记得特斯拉在仪表板中使用标准笔记本电脑屏幕时，因为它们比汽车级便宜，导致故障率很高吗？

> the cost was double because the target market is "ad agencies" or whatever.

A TV capable of operating in those conditions has to be more expensive or else it'll need replacing twice as often and cost even more long term. Remember when Tesla used bog standard laptop screens in their dash because they were cheaper than automotive grade, leading to high failure rate?

xp84 2026/03/26 23:29
这让我想知道我当地的麦当劳在得来速餐厅里垂直安装了三个大屏幕，最终是否没有商业级的屏幕。它们整天在炎热的气候下在阳光下做饭，所以它们会出现故障并变得一团糟，看起来它们的周期大约是 3 个月新更换和工作，1 年闪烁。
This makes me wonder if my local McDonalds, which has three big screens mounted vertically in the drive-thru, ended up with not the commercial grade ones. They’re cooking in the sun in a hot climate all day, so they fail and turn into flickery messes, and it seems like they’re on a cycle of roughly 3 months newly-replaced & working, 1 year flickering.

bombcar 2026/03/27 00:31
它们可能在商业显示器内 - 不是户外额定的，这要贵得多（而且通常看起来更糟糕）。
They’re probably inside commercial displays - not outdoor rated, which are much, much more expensive (and usually look much worse).
在 HN 中查看
Zigurd 2026/03/27 00:48
如果从未建立网络连接，许多（大多数？）“智能”电视将正常工作。其中许多可以设置为通过 HDMI 信号唤醒，例如来自外置流媒体盒的 HDMI 信号。这意味着你可以利用电视内置的臃肿软件所支付的补贴，我打赌这个价格恰好接近于无补贴的哑电视的价格差异。
Many (most?) "smart" TVs will work fine if their network connection is never set up. Many of those can be set to wake on an HDMI signal from, for example, an outboard streamer box. That means you can take advantage of the subsidy paid by the bloatware that comes inside your TV, for a price that I bet is coincidentally close to the same as the price difference of an unsubsidized dumb TV.
在 HN 中查看
nunez 2026/03/27 01:21
几年前，当我购买新电视时，我也想知道这个问题。不幸的是，不仅很难找到一台“愚蠢”的现代电视，而且最好的显示面板总是在智能电视上，因为这才是卖点。

也就是说，我最终得到了一台索尼​​ A95K 55 英寸电视，它非常棒。它内置了 Google TV，但我立即将我们的 Apple TV 连接到它，从那以后它就再也没有见过互联网。也没有麻烦。索尼还让禁用运动插值变得非常容易，这是我非常不喜欢的功能。

I wondered this as well when I was shopping for a new TV a few years ago. Unfortunately, not only is it really difficult to find a "dumb" modern TV, but the best display panels will always be on smart TVs because that's what sells.

That said, I ended up getting a Sony A95K 55" TV, and it's been great. It has Google TV built-in, but I immediately connected our Apple TV to it, and it's never seen the Internet since. No nags, either. Sony also made it really easy to disable motion interpolation, a feature I really dislike.

nlawalker 2026/03/26 23:57
有一段时间，Costco 享有盛誉，因为你可以在这里购买电视，并确信它可以作为“哑巴”电视使用。有传言（据我所知未经证实）是，在制造商为零售商特定型号进行的定制中，Costco 的定制包括固件调整，以降低对强制连接、帐户创建等要求。

我不确定这是否属实，但无论如何，Costco 仍然享有退货电视方便的声誉，而且他们会注意所声明的退货原因。

For a while, Costco had a reputation as the place where you could buy a TV and be confident that it was usable as a "dumb" TV. The rumor (unconfirmed as far as I know) was that, among the customizations that manufacturers would make for retailer-specific models, the Costco ones included firmware tweaks to pull back on requirements for things like mandatory connectivity, account creation and the like.

I'm not sure how true any of that is, but in any case Costco still has a reputation as a place where it's easy to return a TV, and they pay attention to the stated reason for return.

“除非你在手机上下载应用程序、创建帐户并登录电视，否则它不会让你做任何事情。你不能只是将其更改为 HDMI 1 并使用 Firestick。我用一封废弃的电子邮件进行了设置，然后删除了该应用程序，并将电视从 WiFi 上断开。”

sdoering 2026/03/27 01:07
他们预先告诉你这一点吗？你什么时候买？我曾经退回过一个小型蓝牙扬声器，因为它不能让我连接到它。它希望我下载一个应用程序，创建一个帐户，登录，然后接受一大堆关于他们如何收集各种数据的废话（在这种情况下，我妈妈对将流式传输到扬声器的内容的消费）。

那是一个该死的便携式蓝牙音箱。好吧，一小时内就被退回了，竞争对手是在网上购买的。

至少，商店接受了它——如果他们不接受的话，我会立即将其作为案例发送给消费者保护机构。因为他们甚至从未暗示过（无论是在包装上还是在商店中）该应用程序和废话的必要性。

Do they tell you this up front? When you buy it? I once returned a small bluetooth speaker, because it would not just let me connect to it. It wanted me to download an app, create an account, sign in and then accept a metric ton of crap about how they would collect all kinds of data about (in this case my mom's consumption of content that would be streamed to the speaker).

It was a damn portable bluetooth speaker. Well it was returned within an hour and the competitor was bought online.

At least, the store accepted it - I would have instantly send this as a case to the consumer protection agency, if they hadn't, though. Because they never even hinted (neither on the packaging, nor in the store) towards the necessity of the app and crap.

The local electronics chain has 50-100 different TV's. All are 'smart'.

shermantanktop 2026/03/26 22:45

h2zizzle 2026/03/26 23:07
对于那些被迫观看 NBC 转播的奥运会开幕式和闭幕式的人来说，我们已经生活在哈里森·伯杰龙的世界中。 “不要对艺术产生未经批准的想法，或者忘记我们的商业赞助商！”

在 HN 中查看
abenga 2026/03/27 10:31
我记得 2010 年奥运会是在预先加密的 Youtube 上播出的（在一些国家）。您可以点击图标进入不同活动的直播，零广告，无需切换演播室播音员（留在现场），零大惊小怪。这是光荣的。

我觉得生活在一个广告商不关心的国家会让你的数字生活变得更好。例如，NBA联盟通行证在我国所有球队都很便宜。我很乐意每月支付一笔费用，我可以按需观看所有球队的所有比赛，而不会出现停电。在比赛休息期间，我们要么观看体育场直播、动画 NBA 标志，要么观看其他比赛的精彩集锦和倒计时。

I remember the 2010 Olympics were aired on pre-enshittified Youtube (in a few countries). You could click on icons to go to a live stream of different events, zero ads, no cutting to studio announcers (stayed on the fields), zero fuss. It was glorious.

I feel like living in a country advertisers don't care about makes your digital life better. For example the NBA league pass is very cheap in my country, all teams. For an amount I'd be glad to pay per month, I can watch all games for all teams on demand, without blackouts. And during game breaks we either watch the stadium feed, an animated NBA logo, or highlights and countdowns of other games.

autoexec 2026/03/27 00:08
彻底过滤广告永远不会发生。他们会创建一个市场，设备会将您看到或听到的广告替换为出价最高者的广告，然后在其上插入新广告。

nla 2026/03/26 23:34
这就是为什么您从未将“智能电视”连接到互联网的原因。 如果您不同意，请执行此操作，然后运行 ​​Wireshark 分析。 在尝试了所有方法之后，我发现最好的选择是 Apple TV。 遥测最少，图像质量最好，而且非常安全。
This is why you never connected a "smart TV" to the internet. If you disagree, do it and then run a Wireshark analysis. The best option I have found, after trying them all is Apple TV. Least about of telemetry, best picture quality, and very secure.
在 HN 中查看
RiverCrochet 2026/03/26 23:07
很多人在沃尔玛购买杂货（即使你不这样做）。这使得沃尔玛能够在电视上向人们提供食品和其他日常用品的折扣。人们会喜欢这个——尤其是那些会在沃尔玛购买廉价电视的人。他们真的很想在杂货或汽油上节省几美元。更不用说沃尔玛现在可以通过电视向其数百万员工提供福利。他们也会喜欢的。

沃尔玛是有史以来诉讼最多的公司之一，在任何特定时间可能都有 10 多起针对它的活跃诉讼。因此，如果他们参与其中，他们相当确定它现在和将来都会合法运作。

反对电视默认收集个人数据的斗争此时可能已经失败。结束了。不久之后，非智能电视可能会成为大多数人无法承受的专业化、超昂贵的企业级消费。

投影仪能够像电视一样在墙上产生大图像，虽然亮度不高，但对隐私的侵犯要少得多，而且还便于携带。这就是我未来可能会花掉我的电视收入的地方，直到那些人也陷入其中。

A lot of people do their grocery shopping at Walmart (even if you don't). This positions Walmart as being able to offer discounts for food and other daily necessities to people right on their TV. People are going to like this-especially the cohort that would buy a cheap TV at Walmart. They're going to really like saving a few dollars on groceries or gas. Not to mention Walmart can now offer perks through the TV to its millions of employees. They're going to like it too.

Walmart is one of the most litigated companies ever, and probably has 10+ active lawsuits against it at any given time. So if they're getting into this, they're fairly sure it will work legally now and in the future.

The battle against personal-data-collection by default on TVs is probably lost at this point. It's over. Non-smart TVs will probably become specialized, super-expensive corporate-class expenses out of reach of most people before too long.

Projectors are capable of creating a big image on a wall like a TV, and while it's not as bright, it comes with much less privacy invasion, and is also portable. That's where I'm likely spending my future TV dollars until those gets caught up in this as well.

在 HN 中查看
waltwalther 2026/03/26 23:24
很棒的一点！ 我的本能反应是，这是一种入侵，最终用户将被扣为人质，除非他/她向沃尔玛放弃个人信息……也许对某些人来说就是这种情况，但有些人肯定会从个人广告和折扣中受益。 我确实相信电视包装​​外面应该有一个大的、明亮的、不可避免的通知，说明需要沃尔玛帐户才能使用电视。
Great point! My knee-jerk reaction was that this is an intrusion and the enduser would be held hostage unless he/she gives up personal information to Walmart...and maybe that is the case for some, but some will surely benefit from the personal advertising and discounts. I do believe there should be a large, bright, unavoidable notice on the outside of the TV packaging stating that a Walmart account is required to use the TV.
在 HN 中查看
RiverCrochet 2026/03/26 23:33
这也将使沃尔玛受益。他们有 Walmart+，这是他们的杂货配送和店内结账应用程序 - 如果您曾经在城市附近繁忙的沃尔玛购物，这两个应用程序都可以让您避免实际进入沃尔玛，或者如果您进入商店，可以更快地进入。

因此，该贴纸将是一个很大的“这台电视需要 Walmart+ 帐户 - 注册 Walmart+，订单超过 30 美元即可享受免费杂货送货服务，并可在自助结账时享受折扣，并在流媒体上享受优惠！”他们的电子部门人员可能会接受培训来回答任何问题并帮助人们在应用程序上注册（如果他们还没有注册）。

沃尔玛在这方面非常聪明。

And that will also benefit Walmart. They have Walmart+ which is their grocery delivery and in-store checkout app - which, if you've ever shopped at a busy Walmart near a city, both of those either enable you to avoid actually entering a Walmart or make it much quicker if you go in the store.

So that sticker will be a big "This TV requires a Walmart+ account - Sign up for Walmart+ and get free grocery delivery on orders over $30 and discounts at the self-checkout AND deals on streaming!" Their electronics department people will probably be trained to answer any questions and help people sign up on the app (if they're not already).

Walmart's pretty smart here.

RiverCrochet 2026/03/26 23:18
我敢打赌，五年之内，电视需要连接互联网才能使用将变得司空见惯。一是ATSC 3.0及其DRM加密功能。另一种情况可能是，由于电视预装了应用程序，因此电视必须记录您的年龄并向上游注册，以遵守年龄验证法或其解释。
I would bet five figures that within 5 years it will be commonplace for TVs to require an Internet connection in order to be used at all. One is ATSC 3.0 and its DRM encryption capabilities. The other scenario is probably be that, because the TV has pre-installed applications, then the TV has to record your age and register it upstream to comply with an age-verification law or interpretation thereof.

spl757 2026/03/27 08:19
多年来，Vizio 和其他所有主要品牌一直在从智能电视中收集数据。如果您必须登录，那么跟踪您正在做的事情会容易得多。

在这一点上，实际上，将各个部件拼凑起来会更容易，而不是花钱购买一台带有 Tizen 操作系统的电视，电视上可能有一个摄像头，可以看到你坐在沙发上，手指因奇多狂吃而变成橙色，对着电视尖叫，因为这种情况现在似乎经常发生。

1984 年已经过去 10 年了，现在它已经足够大到引人注目了。

Vizio and every other major brand has been, for years, harvesting data from your smart TV. It's a lot easier to track what you are doing if you have to be logged in.

At this point, it can actually be easier to piece parts together instead of paying for a TV with Tizen OS on a tv that may have a camera to watch you sit on the couch with your fingers all orange from the cheeto binge screaming at the TV because that seems to happen a lot now.

1984 has been here for 10 years, now it's just grown big enough to be noticable.

在 HN 中查看
bob1029 2026/03/26 23:19
我解决这个问题的方法是停止使用电视。

我实际上比 PC/Mac 显示器更喜欢的最后一台电视是一台巨大的松下等离子电视，它有一些严重的实用性问题。我每周可能会使用它 2-3 小时。

我对其他显示技术都没有那么感兴趣。 OLED 有时会很接近，但没有什么能比得上主频为 600hz 的等离子面板的内心紧迫感。这 噪音 它会在明亮的场景中变得疯狂。你必须有一个强大的声音系统来掩盖半导体开关。而且，这对我来说就是电视的全部意义所在。要么做大，要么回家。展览。如果我只是想消费内容而不惹恼邻居，我的 MacBook/PC 往往会提供更好的体验。

My solution to this problem has been to stop using TVs.

The last TV I actually enjoyed more than a PC/Mac display was a monster Panasonic plasma that had some serious practicality issues. I'd use it maybe 2-3 hours per week.

None of the other display technologies are that interesting to me. OLED gets close sometimes, but nothing matches the visceral urgency of a plasma panel clocked at 600hz. The noises it would make in bright scenes was crazy. You had to have a powerful sound system to cover up the semiconductor switching. And, that's kind of the entire point for me with a television. Go big or go home. Exhibition. If I just want to consume content without pissing off the neighbors, my MacBook/PC tends to provide a much better experience.

bombcar 2026/03/26 23:15
与一次性 Vizios 相比，它们的价格高得离谱——为会议租用一台“真正的”显示器需要 1000 美元左右（包括运输、安装、退货，通常涉及大板条箱和卡车）。

购买类似的 Vizio，沃尔玛会以 200 美元的价格免费送货，你只需忽略设置提示，插入 HDMI，完成后将其交给一位友善的酒店员工。

They are insanely priced compared to disposable Vizios - renting a "real" display monitor for a conference would be $1000 or so (including the shipping, setup, return, usually involving large crates and trucks).

Buying a similar Vizio would be delivered by free by Walmart for $200, you just ignore the setup prompts, stick HDMI in, and give it to a nice hotel employee when done.

SunshineTheCat 2026/03/26 22:45
这是一个我从未真正考虑过的疯狂想法，但绝对有可能：使用电视作为亏本产品来销售广告。

不确定这是否是他们的意图，但我可以很容易地看到这正在成为一件事（如果还没有的话）。还有什么比强迫人们创建帐户然后跟踪他们对设备的使用情况更好的收集有关人们的有用广告数据的方法呢？

Kinda a wild idea I had never really considered, but absolutely a possibility: using a TV as a loss leader to sell ads.

Not sure if that's they're intent here, but I could easily see that becoming a thing (if it isn't already). And what better way to collect useful ad data on people than forcing them to create an account and then tracking their usage of the device.

在 HN 中查看
mholm 2026/03/26 22:50
这是十年来的标准。如果没有第二个利润来源，200 美元买一台 4k 55 英寸电视是不现实的
That's been the standard for a decade. $200 for a 4k 55" TV is not realistic without a secondary profit source
在 HN 中查看
mikepurvis 2026/03/26 23:04
计算机显示器价格昂贵的部分原因是。它们是密度更高、刷新率更高的面板，但它们也 哑的 因此标价必须涵盖全部成本；遥控器上没有“netflix”按钮，可以贡献几块钱或其他什么。
Part of the reason that computer monitors are considerably more expensive. They're higher density, higher refresh rate panels, but they're also dumb so the sticker price has to cover the whole cost; no "netflix" button on the remote control contributing a few bucks or whatever.
在 HN 中查看
andrewmunsell 2026/03/26 23:00

This has been a thing for years, and so much so, that there's an entire TV with a dedicated second screen that shows you ads underneath your main screen: https://www.telly.com

ifyoubuildit 2026/03/26 23:09
哇。我想我很惊讶横幅广告花了这么长时间才到达电视。

如果您想要更便宜的，我们可以在您的广告拦截器上打印我们自己的广告！

Wow. I guess I'm surprised it took this long for banner ads to reach TVs.

I think I have my next startup idea: a physical ad blocker for this thing. We could even have multiple styles: yellow sticky note, duct tape, painters tape.

And if you want cheaper ones, we can print our own ads on your ad blocker!

在 HN 中查看
beloch 2026/03/26 23:38
“沃尔玛发言人向 Ars Technica 证实，“部分新的 Vizio OS 电视”将强制要求拥有沃尔玛帐户，以便业主完成注册并使用智能电视功能。”

------

潜在买家应该问的一些问题：

1、这款“智能电视”是否需要“入职”才能充当“哑屏”？ 即用户是否需要获得沃尔玛帐户才能访问视频设置？

2. 是否会注入广告或 Phone Home 来共享 HDMI 输入的屏幕截图？

3. 让它访问 WiFi 还不够吗，或者这个东西有其他获取“更新”的方法吗？

"A Walmart spokesperson confirmed to Ars Technica that Walmart accounts will be mandatory on “select new Vizio OS TVs” for owners to complete onboarding and to use smart TV features."

------

Some questions prospective buyers should ask:

1. Is "onboarding" necessary for this "Smart TV" to function as a "dumb screen"? i.e. Would a user need to get a Walmart account just to access video settings?

2. Does it inject ads or phone home to share screen captures from HDMI input?

3. Is not giving it access to WiFi sufficient, or does this thing have alternative ways of getting "updates"?

在 HN 中查看

Is this a benefit in disguise? You refuse to create walmart account, and therefore your smart TV is rendered dumb and you can just use it like normal?

greybeardgeek 2026/03/27 00:11
最近，我为年迈的妈妈购买了两台电视，她永远不会观看流媒体或使用应用程序。我只看那些有在线手册供审查的电视。我找到了两个 LG，我可以禁用应用程序启动屏幕，并设置为“选择开机时的最后一个输入”，在她的例子中，它是 HDMI-1 上的有线电视盒。不知道这样的选择还能持续多久。
Recently I sourced two TVs for my elderly mom, who is never going to stream or use an app. I only looked at TVs which had their manuals online for review. I found two LGs which I could disable the app splash screen, and set to "select last input on power-up", which in her case is a cable box on HDMI-1. Don't know how long choices such as this will be available.

nazgulsenpai 2026/03/27 00:49
我有一台海信 Roku 电视，带有 pihole 网络。它无法到达任何广告网络，因此该接口始终是默认接口。请注意，默认设置不是很好，但它是无广告的。
I have a Hisense Roku TV with on a network with a pihole. It can't reach any of it's ad networks so the interface is always the default. The default isn't great, mind you, but it is ad-free.

ProllyInfamous 2026/03/27 01:01
只是出于好奇：为什么将电视添加到您的网络 只是为了阻止它上网？

也许您将内容流式传输到本地？

我同时拥有 PiHole 和 SmartTV——后者甚至从未获得过 WiFi 接入（只是将它们用作 CCTV 和 PS4 的显示器）。

Just out of curiosity: why add the TV to your network just to block it from the internet?

Perhaps you stream content to it locally?

I have both a PiHole and SmartTVs — the latter never even get WiFi access (just use them as displays for CCTV & PS4).

在 HN 中查看
nazgulsenpai 2026/03/27 02:44
这更容易，我的动机是阻止广告。我们也有 PS4，PS4 上显示的广告比被阉割的 Roku 多得多。

在 HN 中查看
ProllyInfamous 2026/03/27 02:47
我也不连接 PS4 — 但如果您的 DHCP 服务器向 DNS 发出了 PiHole 的地址，那么您可能可以专门阻止该广告服务器（就像您对 Roku/Vizio 所做的那样）。

想知道电视制造商什么时候开始 要求 使用的互联网连接，甚至可能通过 https 地址硬编码 DNS。

>【将电视连接WiFi更简单】

为什么？它使什么变得更容易 适合您的设置？

I also don't connect PS4 — but if you have your DHCP server issue your PiHole's address for DNS, then you can probably block that ad server specifically (just like you could for Roku/Vizio).

Wondering when the TV manufacturers begin requiring an internet connection to u se, perhaps even hard-coding DNS-via-https addresses.

>It's just easier [to connect TV to WiFi]

why? What does it make easier for your setup?

nazgulsenpai 2026/03/27 03:00
我不需要做任何额外的事情。我的网络上有一个 pihole 拦截广告，我有一台 Roku 电视，内置了从流媒体服务进行流媒体播放的功能。我将电视连接到 Wifi，我的目标就实现了，即拥有一台没有广告的流媒体电视。
I don't have to do anything extra. I have a pihole on my network blocking ads, I have a Roku TV that has the ability built-in to stream from streaming services. I connect the TV to Wifi and my goal of having a streaming TV without ads littering the interface was achieved.

-warren 2026/03/26 23:44
这就是为什么我有 protonmail 别名、一次性第二部手机和 x1 虚拟抄送。虽然我无法阻止这种混乱，但我可以通过保持伪匿名并划分每项服务来进行反击。

eks391 2026/03/27 07:05
您所描述的是别名，而不是分隔。为了有效地划分每项服务需要不同的电子邮件、电话和抄送。通过每个别名只有一个别名，与合作伙伴共享有关您的信息的广告网络在看到多个供应商的相同质子电子邮件等时，可以毫无障碍地将您的习惯联系起来，即使您的替代产品有不同的名称。在这些信息被匹配足够多次之后，就会有足够的个人资料来有效地了解您是谁，即使这只是可能的而不是经过证实的。 您提到了质子，它允许在一个付费帐户上发送多封电子邮件，并且有几个竞争对手提供类似的服务，并取得了不同程度的成功，因此实施起来应该没有问题。也有一些公司提供匿名抄送，尽管它们慢慢不起作用了。 Privacy 和 Ironvest 是两个曾经有效的工具，但我不确定它们是否仍然有效。如果您知道大部分时间仍在供应商中工作的 CC 公司，我会很感兴趣。关于电话，也有多种来源，具体取决于您是想要长期使用号码还是只需要一次性使用号码，但随着安全措施的增加，这些来源同样已经失败。
What you described is aliasing, not compartmentalization. To effectively compartmentalize requires a different email, phone, and CC for every service. By only having one alias of each, the ad networks that are sharing information about you with partners have no obstacles tying together your habits when they see the same proton email, etc across several vendors, even if you have a different name on your alternate products. After these have been matched enough times, there enough of a profile on you to effectively know who you are, even if it is just probable instead of proven. You mention proton, which allows several emails on a paid account, and there are several competitors offering a similar service with varying success, so this should be no problem to implement. There are also companies that offer anonymous CCs, although they are slowly not working. Privacy and Ironvest are two that used to work but I'm not sure if they still do. I'd be interested if you know of CC companies that still work most of the time at vendors. Regarding phones, there are several sources for this as well, depending on whether you want long term or just one time use numbers, but these likewise have been failing as security measures increase.

电视智能功能几乎总是功能不足的间谍软件，因此电视无法使用 Wi-Fi 将其用作哑显示器和 BYOSTV。

如果您想要更少/不同的间谍软件，可以选择 Fire TV Stick 4K Max* 35 美元、Apple TV 4K* 149 美元或 Nvidia Shield TV Pro** 199 美元。

* 显示广告

** 显示可通过停止 Google/Android 服务来禁用的广告

TV smart features are almost always underpowered spyware, so no Wi-Fi for the TV to use it as a dumb display and BYOSTV.

If one wanted arguably less/different spyware, there's the Fire TV Stick 4K Max* $35, Apple TV 4K* $149, or Nvidia Shield TV Pro** for $199.

* Shows ads

** Shows ads that can be disabled by stopping Google/Android services

dylan604 2026/03/26 23:17
我从不去沃尔玛购物来做到这一点。一个周末，我很无聊，去了一堆商店比较价格。我附近的沃尔玛确实有更便宜的东西，但大部分价格与其他商店平均。所以我可以诚实地说，对于我买的东西，沃尔玛对我来说不值得，这不仅仅是不去沃尔玛购物的原则，而是一个主要问题
I do that by never shopping at Walmart. I was bored one weekend, and went to a bunch of stores to compare prices. The Walmart near me did have somethings that were cheaper, but for the most part the prices were average with other stores. So I can honestly say for things I buy, Walmart is not worth it for me and it's not just the principle of not shopping at Walmart but it's a principal issue

ewoodrich 2026/03/27 00:36
如果您只需从 Play 商店安装免费的 Projectivy 启动器并卸载任何未使用的电视应用程序，Onn 电视棒（基于 Google TV）的价值令人难以置信（25 美元的中间版本是目前最划算的价格），可提供臃肿/无广告的体验。

我刚刚安装了 Stremio、Jellyfin 和 VLC，并将遥控器上的免费电视按钮重新映射到 Stremio。

The Onn TV sticks (Google TV based) are an incredible value (the middle of the line-up $25 version is the best deal currently) for a bloat/ad-free experience if you just install the free Projectivy launcher from the Play Store and uninstall any unused TV apps.

I just have Stremio, Jellyfin and VLC installed and remapped the free TV button on the remote to Stremio.

jgerrish 2026/03/27 01:02
在每个智能电视线程中都有数十或数百条评论表示人们不会将电视连接到无线网络。

我明白。 几十年来我一直是 EFF 的支持者，至少在我有能力的时候是这样。 我得到它。

但问题是您有时想从手机上截屏视频或歌曲。你脏兮兮的手机已经在野外收集恶意软件了。 或者有客人这样做。 当然，什么是视频？

当然，您的电视没有最新的更新。

当恶意软件“泄露”和爆发时，这真是一种不正当的激励措施，将导致对互联网和物联网的更多监管。

在这种情况下，情报部门利用博弈论和对公地悲剧的聪明知识，利用了公民自由主义者。

而不仅仅是教育公民和民主辩论。

我现在没有政治权力来推销这个想法，而且我知道这听起来很疯狂。

那好吧。 我想知道接下来我不能卖什么。

In every Smart TV thread there are dozens or hundreds of comments that say people don't put connect their TV to their wireless.

I understand. I've been an EFF supporter for decades, at least when I can afford it. I get it.

The problem though is that you sometimes want to screencast a video or song from your phone. Your dirty dirty phone that has been out in the wild collecting malware. Or a guest does. And sure, what's one video?

And of course your TV doesn't have the latest updates.

It's a fucking perverse incentive that will lead to more regulation of the Internet and IoT when we get malware "leaks" and outbreaks.

The civil libertarians have been co-opted with game theory and smart knowledge of the tragedy of the commons in this case by the intelligence services.

Instead of just educating citizens and democratic debate.

I don't have the political power to sell that idea now, and I know it sounds crazy.

Oh well. I wonder what I can't sell next.
